Caveats:
1. Statistics are suppressed to protect personal privacy, for the following conditions: <ol><li> Data are suppressed if the

figures represent fewer than 16 cases for the specific category in the table cell.
  • Counts and rates are suppressed for
cells at the Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) level for the race categories "American Indian or Alaska Native" or "Asian

or Pacific Islander" when the specific category's population for rate calculations is less than 50,000 persons.
  • Data
are suppressed at the state and MSA level for certain race and ethnicity groups: <ul><li> American Indian or Alaska Native data

are suppressed at the state and MSA level for residents of Illinois, Kansas, New Jersey, and New York;
  • Hispanic Asian
or Pacific Islander and Hispanic Black or African American data are suppressed at the state and MSA level for all years and for
Non-Hispanic Asian or Pacific Islanders for 1999 for residents of Kansas. States decide whether to
suppress data for specific race and ethnicity categories. The suppression criteria may vary in previous releases of these data
More information: http://wonder.cdc.gov/wonder/help/cancer-v2022.html#Confidentiality.
2. The "Show Totals" checkbox is disabled when data are grouped by Cancer Sites or by Childhood Cancer, because aggregate
values are displayed in the table. Also be aware that charts and maps containing both aggregate and detail data could be
misleading.
3. Age-adjusted rates are not available when results are grouped by age.
4. Data are from selected statewide and metropolitan area cancer registries that meet data quality criteria. Incidence data
shown for the United States are diagnoses and populations from included registries only.
5. Information on primary site, behavior, and histology was coded according to the International Classification of Diseases for
Oncology, Third Edition (ICD-O-3) and categorized according to the revised SEER recodes dated March 1, 2018, which define
standard groupings of primary cancer sites.
6. For the 2005 year, the Census Bureau estimates that 203,937 persons were displaced from Alabama, Louisiana, Mississippi and
Texas due to Hurricanes Katrina and Rita. CDC WONDER does not include the displaced persons in the 2005 population counts for
these states, nor are these counts included in the summary populations for the affected division, regions or national
population. However, the USCS web site does include these displaced persons in the national population figures for 2005.
